parentQuestion: A patient cannot detect vibration from a tuning fork placed on their ankle but c...

scaffolds.q: Different mechanoreceptors detect different aspects of touch.
scaffolds.explain: Light touch is detected by Meissner's corpuscles and Merkel discs. Vibration is detected by Pacinian corpuscles. Deep pressure by Ruffini endings. Each receptor is specialized for a specific stimulus feature. Welcome this realization:the division of labor — one mechanical modality, multiple specialized detectors.

scaffolds.q: Pacinian corpuscles are rapidly adapting receptors that detect vibration.
scaffolds.explain: Pacinian corpuscles are encapsulated receptors in deep skin and fascia that respond to high-frequency vibration (40-400 Hz). They adapt rapidly, detecting CHANGES in pressure rather than sustained pressure. Press onward:: they're built for detecting texture during active touch and vibration from external sources. Exquisite specialization.
